title: Retry with Exponential Backoff

problem.statement: call_with_retry(api_call, backoff_fn, max_attempts=3) should retry api_call() up to max_attempts times whenever it raises TimeoutError, calling backoff_fn(attempt) before each retry (but not after the final failed attempt), and re-raising the TimeoutError if every attempt fails. Fill in the except block.
problem.use_case: Retry-with-backoff around a real requests.get()/post() call is standard practice against any API with transient failures or rate limits — getting the 'don't back off after the last attempt, just re-raise' detail right is what separates working retry logic from an infinite-feeling hang.

hints: On the last attempt, there's no point backing off — you're out of retries, so re-raise instead.
A bare 'raise' inside the except block re-raises the exception that's currently being handled.

solution.explanation: On the final attempt, re-raise instead of backing off: if attempt == max_attempts: raise. Otherwise call backoff_fn(attempt) and let the for loop try again — the try block's own 'return api_call()' handles the success path.
